rationale: Spec-coverage lens: every requirement R1-R8 is delivered by exactly the beads the Requirement->bead map claims, and I verified the map is neither over- nor under-assigned. All 12 spec ACs and all 6 Validation Proofs trace to real Verification items in the bead sections (spot-checked each grep/test against the bead that owns it); the nine test-function names match the spec ACs verbatim, and each AC's embedded edge assertions (re-panel co-bump in BOTH panel.json+BRIEF header, prior-round verdicts + skill body untouched, -round-0 negative, bare-name-tolerated, no-coverage-diagnostic) are reproduced in the test descriptions. No scope creep: the BRIEF machine-header mechanism, schema-drift test, doc-sync regions, and instruct refactor are all mandated by the spec (R1/R4/Scope/R2). Grounding confirmed: loadImpactedDomains IS contextpack.ParseSpec; ApproveSpec inherits ValidateSpec failures. All four Non-Goals and all six Open-Question resolutions are respected. One low-severity note (R6 regexp breadth), not blocking.

findings.title: R6 extraction regexp is slightly broader than the spec's pinned 'anchored touchpoint BULLET' rule, but satisfies every R6 falsification and AC9 case
findings.detail: Spec R6 pins the reference-extraction rule to 'only an anchored markdown-link touchpoint bullet -- a bullet of the shape `- [ADR-XXXX](<path>): ...`'. Bead 2 step 2 implements extraction as `\[(ADR-\d{4})\]\([^)]+\)` over the whole `## ADR Touchpoints` section body, which the plan itself describes as 'every [ADR-dddd](<path>) occurrence in that section' -- i.e. any anchored link, not specifically a leading `- [...](...):` citation bullet. For 110's own spec (and the AC9 fixtures) the two rules coincide because every anchored link is a leading bullet, so the plan passes all R6 falsification conditions (bare-prose ignored, anchored-missing rejected, same citation-gate store, no coverage/irrelevant diagnostic) and all three AC9 cases, and 110 self-validates (Bead 2 verification `mindspec validate spec 110-...` exits 0). The only theoretical divergence is an anchored link appearing mid-prose (not as a leading citation bullet) that points to a nonexistent ADR: the plan would flag it, the pinned 'bullet' rule would not. That case is untested and is itself a genuine broken link, so this is defensible latitude rather than a coverage defect; flagging for the record only.

findings.title: R6 uses a distinct diagnostic code `adr-touchpoint-missing`; R5 correctly reuses `impacted-domains-resolve` verbatim
findings.detail: R5 pins its code exactly ('under the SAME impacted-domains-resolve code') and Bead 2 step 1 emits that literal code -- compliant. R6, by contrast, does NOT pin a code name in the spec (only that it mirrors adr-cite-missing's existence-only SHAPE and emits no coverage/irrelevant diagnostic), so Bead 2 step 2's choice of a new `adr-touchpoint-missing` code is within spec latitude, not a violation. Noting only because a reader comparing R5 and R6 might expect symmetric code reuse; the asymmetry is intentional and spec-permitted (the touchpoint check reads the spec's touchpoints prose, not the plan's frontmatter citations).

findings.title: Non-Goals and all six Open-Question resolutions verified respected
findings.detail: Non-Goal 1 (no new decision semantics/gate): verify writes nothing/exits 0, tally exit is reporting only, R7a contract-pinned. Non-Goal 2 (no telemetry): no bead adds metrics. Non-Goal 3 (no stricter semantic validation): Bead 2 checks are deterministic resolver/store runs, no criterion-quality judgment. Non-Goal 4 (no change to how touchpoints/citations are declared): R6 is integrity-check only. OQ1 (ADR-0040 prose, MAY cite in frontmatter): plan frontmatter cites ADR-0040 with ADR Fitness justification. OQ3 (leaf-safe writer, plain values): Bead 1 Create takes plain args, no config/git import. OQ4 (tally prints union, skill authors consolidated): Bead 4 union + Bead 5 keeps Consolidate. OQ5 (checks in ValidateSpec): confirmed. OQ6 (instruct-template gen deferred): Out of Scope, untouched.
